title: tetraplasandra meiandra
intro: Tetraplasandra meiandra, also known as the Tahitian copperleaf or meiandra, is a plant species in the family Cestrum, native to the island of Tahiti in French Polynesia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits of Tetraplasandra meiandra include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against oxidative stress, which may help to prevent chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, Tetraplasandra meiandra is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
